texto: I realised I never wanted to leave her side, and I never wanted her to have to struggle to be released from her sadness. I could never suggest she required release; for she did not require release. It was a true thing, this sadness of hers; a true thing about the world, about life, about herself. I just wanted Daphne. I wanted who she was, how she was, only her, all of her, always. And I knew I would be forever treading the long path towards that shrouded chamber of dusky luminance I glimpsed in her flickering sort of half-smiles.
explicacao: Orientação direta de R.M. Miller sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “I realised I never wanted to leave her side, and I never wanted her to have to struggle to be rel…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, love, melancholy, útil em momentos de melancholy e sadness.
